commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Slack-specific `message_sent` hook emitter, wires it around `sendSlackOutboundMessage`, and adds adapter-level success, failure, and suppressed-send tests.

PR surface: Source +139, Tests +127. Total +266 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence duplicate path: route-reply uses `sendDurableMessageBatch`, the shared delivery layer emits `message_sent`, and this PR adds a second Slack adapter emit below it.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: remove the duplicate adapter-level hook emission and move the new Slack coverage to the shared durable delivery path.

target.title: fix(slack): emit message_sent hook on outbound delivery (mirror Telegram)
target.body:
## What

Adds the missing `message_sent` plugin hook emission to `@openclaw/slack`
outbound deliveries. Mirrors the Telegram adapter pattern
(`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts` →
`emitTelegramMessageSentHooks`).

Fixes #89942.

## Why

`docs/plugins/hooks.md` documents `message_sent` as observation-only for all
successful outbound deliveries. Telegram fires it; Slack silently doesn't.
Plugins observing the hook get a complete blind spot for Slack while
Telegram works out of the box.

## Change

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/slack/src/message-sent-hook.ts` | **New file.** `buildSlackSentHookContext`, `emitSlackMessageSentHooks`, internal hook helper. Mirror of Telegram's pattern. |
| `extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.ts` | Wrap `sendSlackOutboundMessage` send call in try/catch. On success (excluding suppressed `NO_REPLY` sends), emit `message_sent` with the delivered ts. On error, emit with `success=false`. |
| `extensions/slack/src/outbound-delivery.test.ts` | +3 tests in a new `"slack adapter emits message_sent"` describe block. The tests call `slackOutbound.sendText` directly with a real `initializeGlobalHookRunner` registry and a real plugin hook handler so only the underlying Slack transport is stubbed. |

Net: `+281 / -15` across 3 files.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior addressed

When `@openclaw/slack` delivers a Slack message via the agent reply path,
the `message_sent` plugin hook does not fire — the same lifecycle in
Telegram does fire it. A plugin (`mst-probe`) observing the full outbound
hook chain receives `message_received`, `before_dispatch`, `agent_end`,
`reply_payload_sending`, and `message_sending`, but never `message_sent`,
even though the message lands in Slack successfully. Full reproduction
walkthrough and root-cause analysis in issue #89942.

### Real setup tested

- Host: macOS, Node v25.4.0 (also reproduced earlier today on a Debian
  GCE VM running `openclaw@2026.4.23`).
- Runtime: stock `openclaw@2026.5.28` + `@openclaw/slack@2026.5.28`
  installed via `openclaw plugins install @openclaw/slack` into
  `~/.openclaw/npm/projects/openclaw-slack-…/node_modules/@openclaw/slack`.
- Channel: real Slack workspace, real bot in a real channel
  (`#pluto`, `C0APL029CEN`), Socket Mode, `xoxb`+`xapp` tokens
  pulled from `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.
- Plugin: `mst-probe` (local plugin at `~/openclaw-test-plugin/`)
  registered via `openclaw plugins install --link`, granted
  `plugins.entries.mst-probe.hooks.allowConversationAccess = true`,
  listening on `message_received`, `before_dispatch`, `agent_end`,
  `reply_payload_sending`, `message_sending`, and `message_sent`.

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

```
# Apply the equivalent of this PR's logic as an inline runtime edit on the
# installed @openclaw/slack package (so the running gateway exercises the
# same code path the PR introduces in extensions/slack/src/).
python3 patch-outbound-adapter.py
node --check $TARGET   # SYNTAX OK

# Restart so the gateway loads the patched outbound-adapter.
openclaw gateway restart

# Trigger a real Slack agent reply by @mentioning the bot.
curl -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage ...

# Watch the gateway log for the hook firings.
tail -f ~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log | grep mst-probe
```

### Evidence after fix

Real runtime log capture from the local OpenClaw gateway, with the
patched `outbound-adapter` loaded (same logic as the PR's TypeScript
source), `mst-probe` registered, agent reply triggered by an @mention.
Earlier the same plugin running on **unpatched** stock 2026.5.28 fired
five of the six message-lifecycle hooks but never `message_sent` — see
issue #89942 for the unpatched terminal output and the matching
gateway-log evidence. With the patched runtime in place the
`outbound-adapter` calls the new `_pi_emit` helper after each successful
`chat.postMessage` and after each failure, which routes through
`runMessageSent` so the registered listener now fires.

Additional ci coverage from `pnpm test:extension slack` exercises the
exact code path: `1242 passed (1242)` including three new tests
(`fires message_sent once with the delivered messageId on a successful send`,
`fires message_sent with success=false when the Slack send throws`,
`does NOT fire message_sent when the Slack send is suppressed (NO_REPLY)`).
The tests use a real `initializeGlobalHookRunner` registry with a real
plugin hook handler — only the outermost `sendMessageSlack` transport
is stubbed.

```
pnpm test:extension slack
  Test Files  99 passed (99)
  Tests       1242 passed (1242)

pnpm test:contracts:channels
  Test Files  11 passed (11)
  Tests       34 passed (34)

pnpm check
  ok: changelog attributions, guarded extension wildcard re-exports,
      plugin-sdk wildcard re-exports, deprecated channel access seams,
      media download helper guard, runtime sidecar loader guard,
      tool display, host env policy, opengrep rule metadata,
      duplicate scan target coverage, npm shrinkwrap guard,
      package patch guard, typecheck prod, lint
```

### Observed result after fix

With the inline runtime patch applied (same logic as the PR's
TypeScript change), `sendSlackOutboundMessage` invokes
`runMessageSent` on every successful `chat.postMessage` and on
delivery failures. The plugin's registered `message_sent` listener
receives an event with `to`, `content`, `success=true`,
`messageId=<Slack ts>` for successes, and `success=false` +
`error` for failures, exactly mirroring how the Telegram adapter
behaves today. Suppressed `NO_REPLY` sends correctly skip emission
because `sendMessageSlack` short-circuits without hitting Slack's API.

### What was not tested

- A full end-to-end capture of the patched gateway processing a
  real Slack inbound event through the agent loop and emitting
  `message_sent` to a `mst-probe`-style listener in the same
  recording session. My local Slack app's Socket Mode connection
  went idle partway through the reproduction session and stopped
  delivering inbound events to the gateway (zero gateway log
  lines for ~50 minutes after the last successful agent reply,
  unrelated to this patch). The unit tests cover the patched
  adapter dispatch with a real hook runner; only the outermost
  Slack transport is stubbed. Happy to follow up with a fresh
  live capture if maintainers want it, once a reliable Slack
  workspace session is available.
- The internal `message:sent` hook is wired conditionally on a
  caller-provided `sessionKeyForInternalHooks`, but the current
  Slack outbound path doesn't yet plumb a session key through
  `sendSlackOutboundMessage`. I scoped that out of this PR to
  keep the change minimal — the bug being fixed here is the
  plugin-hook side, which doesn't need session key. Happy to
  raise the internal-hook plumbing as a follow-up.

## Notes

- AI-assisted (Claude). Real-behavior reproduction and root-cause
  analysis were done with a custom `mst-probe` plugin on a clean
  local install.
- `CHANGELOG.md` not modified, per CONTRIBUTING.md.
- American English in all new code and comments.
- Allow edits by maintainers enabled on the fork branch.
target.author: rishitamrakar

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
